per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, actually. When those episodes happen, I do get pretty sweaty - like, soaking through my pajamas every night for a few nights now. It's been really uncomfortable and it makes me feel even more exhausted during the day.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, sometimes after one of those episodes, I'll feel a bit nauseous and my stomach will feel upset. It's not constant, but it does happen occasionally after I've been coughing or wheezing. And to make matters worse, I've also had some low-grade fever over the past week, usually around 38.2°C (100.8°F), mostly in the evenings.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yes, I've been experiencing low-grade fever, around 38.2°C (100.8°F), mostly in the evenings, which can be accompanied by some intermittent chills. And like I mentioned earlier, I also get pretty sweaty at night when those episodes happen.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I have to admit that my appetite has been a bit off for the past 5 days. I've just not been feeling like eating much, and even when I do eat, it's not satisfying me like it usually would. As for weight changes, I haven't noticed anything significant, but I have been losing some energy and motivation to engage in activities that I normally enjoy.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response:
As an elementary school teacher, my day is pretty packed. I usually wake up around 6 am, get myself ready for work, and head out the door by 7:30 am to catch the bus or drive to school. During the week, I teach from about 8 am to 3 pm, with a short break in between classes for lunch. When I'm not teaching, I like to stay active by taking walks during my free time or going for a jog on the weekends if the weather permits.

I try to eat a balanced diet, but lately, it's been tough because of my reduced appetite. I've been relying more on easy-to-prepare meals and snacks that don't require much effort. Occasionally, I'll cook something hearty like soup or pasta, but even those dishes haven't been as satisfying as they used to be.

As for exercise, I do try to stay active, but with this cough and breathing issues, it's been a real challenge. When I'm feeling up to it, I might take a short walk around the block or do some light stretching at home, but it's not like my usual routine of going for longer jogs or practicing yoga.

One thing that's been on my mind lately is how this persistent cough and difficulty breathing have affected my social life. I've had to cancel plans with friends and family a few times because I just wasn't feeling up to leaving the house. It's been really frustrating, especially since it feels like my body is betraying me right now.

Overall, my lifestyle has taken a bit of a hit because of these symptoms. I'm hoping that with some proper treatment or guidance from you, I can get back on track and start feeling more like myself again.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer when I was a teenager. It's been a while since then, but it's always stuck with me as a bit of a concern, especially given my current symptoms.
